growth_records.agit: 200 rpm (shaking incubator) for planktonic growth assays; static in anaerobic chamber for co-culture/biofilm
growth_records.cite: Cao et al. The interactive relationship between Fusobacterium necrophorum, Bacteroides pyogenes, and Porphyromonas levii in driving inflammatory uterine disease. Microbiome (2026). DOI:10.1186/s40168-025-02320-6. [PMC12911390]
growth_records.snippet: B. pyogenes (µmax = 0.275 h−1, doubling time = 2.52 h) and P. levii (µmax = 0.265 h−1, doubling time = 2.62 h) grew more slowly, reaching the stationary phase after 24 h
growth_records.notes: Directly reported in text (Results, mono-species growth dynamics). Culture: Hungate anaerobic tubes, 200 rpm shaking, 37C, anaerobic chamber.
